## Issue to Resolve

Ignore block comment inside single line block
Give an singe line block containing a block comment than do not wrap this comment to a multiline block. So code below should be kept unchanged:
```

```

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EOL_COMMENT
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.hasNewLineInClosedRange
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 15→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
 18→
 19→/**
 20→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 21→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 22→ */
 23→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 24→    StandardRule(
 25→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 26→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 27→            setOf(
 28→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 29→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 30→            ),
 31→    ) {
 32→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 33→        node: ASTNode,
 34→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 35→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 36→    ) {
 37→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 38→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 39→                node
 40→                    .prevLeaf()
 41→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 42→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 43→                node
 44→                    .nextLeaf()
 45→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46→
 47→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 48→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 49→            ) {
 50→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 51→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 52→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 53→                    //    some comment
 54→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 55→                    emit(
 56→                        node.startOffset,
 57→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 58→                            "disallowed",
 59→                        false,
 60→                    )
 61→                } else {
 62→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 63→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 64→                    emit(
 65→                        node.startOffset,
 66→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 67→                        false,
 68→                    )
 69→                }
 70→                return
 71→            }
 72→
 73→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 74→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 75→
 76→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 77→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 82→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 85→    ) {
 86→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 87→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 88→            emit(
 89→                startOffset,
 90→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 91→                true,
 92→            )
 93→            if (autoCorrect) {
 94→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
 95→            }
 96→        } else {
 97→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 98→            emit(
 99→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
100→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
101→                false,
102→            )
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
107→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
108→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
109→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
110→        rawRemove()
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
114→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
115→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→    ) {
118→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
119→        if (autoCorrect) {
120→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
125→
126→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
127→}
128→
129→public val COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = CommentWrappingRule().ruleId
130→

1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 8→
 9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
10→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
11→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
12→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
13→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
14→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
15→
16→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
17→
18→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
19→
20→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
21→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
22→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
23→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
24→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
25→
26→### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
27→
28→The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
29→* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
30→* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
31→* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
32→* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
33→* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
34→* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
35→* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
36→* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
37→* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
38→* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
39→* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
40→* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
41→
42→Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
43→
44→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
45→
46→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
47→
48→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
49→
50→#### Experimental rules
51→
52→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
53→
54→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
55→
56→#### EditorConfig
57→
58→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
59→
60→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
61→
62→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
63→
64→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguides. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
65→
66→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
67→
68→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
69→
70→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
71→
72→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
73→
74→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
75→
76→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
77→
78→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
79→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
80→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |

 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 15→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
 18→
 19→/**
 20→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 21→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 22→ */
 23→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 24→    StandardRule(
 25→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 26→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 27→            setOf(
 28→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 29→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 30→            ),
 31→    ) {
 32→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 33→        node: ASTNode,
 34→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 35→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 36→    ) {
 37→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 38→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 39→                node
 40→                    .prevLeaf()
 41→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 42→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 43→                node
 44→                    .nextLeaf()
 45→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46→
 47→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 48→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 49→            ) {
 50→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 51→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 52→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 53→                    //    some comment
 54→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 55→                    emit(
 56→                        node.startOffset,
 57→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 58→                            "disallowed",
 59→                        false,
 60→                    )
 61→                } else {
 62→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 63→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 64→                    emit(
 65→                        node.startOffset,
 66→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 67→                        false,
 68→                    )
 69→                }
 70→                return
 71→            }
 72→
 73→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 74→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 75→
 76→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 77→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 82→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 85→    ) {
 86→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 87→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 88→            emit(
 89→                startOffset,
 90→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 91→                true,
 92→            )
 93→            if (autoCorrect) {
 94→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
 95→            }
 96→        } else {
 97→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 98→            emit(
 99→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
100→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
101→                false,
102→            )
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
107→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
108→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
109→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
110→        rawRemove()
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
114→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
115→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→    ) {
118→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
119→        if (autoCorrect) {
120→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
125→
126→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
127→}
128→
129→public val COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = CommentWrappingRule().ruleId

1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 8→
 9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
10→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
11→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
12→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
13→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
14→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
15→
16→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
17→
18→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
19→
20→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
21→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
22→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
23→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
24→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
25→
26→### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
27→
28→The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
29→* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
30→* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
31→* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
32→* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
33→* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
34→* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
35→* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
36→* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
37→* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
38→* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
39→* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
40→* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
41→
42→Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
43→
44→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
45→
46→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
47→
48→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
49→
50→#### Experimental rules
51→
52→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
53→
54→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
55→
56→#### EditorConfig
57→
58→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
59→
60→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
61→
62→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
63→
64→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguides. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
65→
66→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
67→
68→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
69→
70→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
71→
72→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
73→
74→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
75→
76→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
77→
78→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
79→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
80→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
